subject: Gaming
title: Review Bombing: Weaponized Feedback
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Review Bombing: Weaponized Feedback.

hook.text: Thousands of negative reviews appear overnight—not about game quality, but about a political disagreement.

buildup.text: Review bombing floods a game with low scores to punish developers for decisions unrelated to game quality.

discovery.text: The Last of Us Part II received thousands of zero-score reviews within hours—before players could finish it.

twist.text: Steam now flags suspicious review patterns. Metacritic added delays. But platforms struggle to stop coordinated campaigns.

climax.text: Review bombing corrupts the feedback system that players rely on. Anger drowns out legitimate criticism.

punchline.text: Zero scores before finishing the game. Reviews became weapons, not feedback.

quiz.question: What is review bombing in gaming?
options: Flooding a game with low scores to punish unrelated decisions
